channel.title: Preventative
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from Preventative, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Federal Aid for Lead Cleanup Is Receding. That’s a Problem for Cash-Strapped Cities.
item.description: Congress and the Trump administration are rolling back some lead remediation resources. Case studies of two cities and a state that faced lead contami...

item.title: The Doctor-Approved Plan for Taking a GLP-1 Without Losing Your Muscle
item.description: Lose fat, not your strength—here’s how to do both on a GLP-1

item.title: Even Patients Are Shocked by the Prices Their Insurers Will Pay — And It Costs All of Us
item.description: Health care prices are on the rise, and patients are flummoxed that even insurance companies aren’t doing more to control costs.

item.title: This Zodiac Sign Is Definitely The Most Likely To Flake On Plans
item.description: And it's always last minute.

item.title: Worried About Memory Loss? 7 Studies Suggest This Nutrient May Help
item.description: And how to get it.

item.title: Readers Lean On Congress To Solve Crises in Research and Rehab
item.description: KFF Health News gives readers a chance to comment on a recent batch of stories.

item.title: Treating Primary Hyperoxaluria

item.title: Families Defend Disability Services Amid Medicaid Cuts
item.description: Idaho is positioning to slash Medicaid funding as state lawmakers grapple with the effects of the federal One Big Beautiful Bill Act, which President ...

item.title: Medicaid Is Paying for More Dental Care. GOP Cuts Threaten To Reverse the Trend.
item.description: More than three dozen states cover dental services for low-income and disabled individuals on Medicaid, in recognition of such care’s importance to ov...

item.title: 5 Ways To Address Crepey Skin On Your Neck, Backed By Science & A Derm
item.description: Here's a skin care fact for you: The skin on your neck and chest is some of the most delicate, sensitive, and thinnest of the entire body.

item.title: This Common Factor Could Be Making You Less Motivated To Work Out
item.description: When it comes to exercise, motivation is everything.

item.title: Miriam Paramore, RxUtility
item.description: In this quickbite interview recorded at the Feb 2026 VIVE conference, I am talking with Miriam Paramore. Miriam is building RxUtility, which is helpin...

item.title: Lupito Nyong’o’s Fibroids Have Returned, She Now Has 50
item.description: Last summer, Academy Award-winning actress Lupita Nyong’o revealed that she had 30 fibroids removed via myomectomy not long after winning her Oscar in...

item.title: The Truth About Compounded GLP-1 Drugs
item.description: GLP-1 medications are everywhere these days — especially online. A quick scroll through social media can bring up a wide range of companies and influe...

item.title: ‘Real Housewives of Salt Lake City Star’ Mary M. Cosby’s Son Robert Cosby Jr. Dead At 23

item.title: Breaking Down Graves' Disease Treatment Options
item.description: You might not spend that much time thinking about your thyroid, but it’s doing an awful lot for you. The small butterfly-shaped gland in your neck hel...

item.title: Human Braiding Hair May Be a Health Hazard Too
item.description: If you think switching to human hair for your protective styles is safer for your hair and health, think again. Consumer Reports followed up on the te...

item.title: Hypertension Bites Exposes a Silent Threat
item.description: In 2026, almost half of adults in this country are living with high blood pressure, according to the CDC, and many don’t notice it until it’s too late...

item.title: Jay Harris on Prostate Cancer
